howItWorks.intro: This spice substitution calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Substitute Amount
rules.formula: Substitute Qty = Original Qty x Conversion Ratio
rules.explanation: Each spice pair has a specific ratio based on relative potency and flavor intensity.

rules.name: Fresh to Dried
rules.explanation: Standard conversion: 1 tablespoon fresh herb equals 1 teaspoon dried.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.q: What is the fresh to dried herb ratio?
faq.a: The general rule is 1 tablespoon fresh = 1 teaspoon dried (3:1 ratio). Dried herbs are more concentrated because water has been removed. Add dried herbs early in cooking and fresh herbs at the end.

faq.q: Can I use garlic powder instead of fresh garlic?
faq.a: Yes. Use 1/3 teaspoon garlic powder per clove of fresh garlic. Powder works best in dry rubs, marinades, and sauces. Fresh garlic is better when you want the texture and sharper flavor, like in garlic bread or stir-fry.
